body: Confirmed reproducible. The companion anchor packet fires before the cell load completes on the client side. Will dig into the packet sequence.
Happens every time with Lydia, fast travelling to Whiterun from Riften. No other mods loaded.
Found the issue in the cell transition handler — we're broadcasting position before onCellAttach fires. Patching now.
Known limitation — we only sync the topic selection, not the current subtopic index. Filed for tracking.
Noticed this with both Jarl dialogue and merchant topics. Consistent across sessions.
Even a basic lobby list with player count and ping would be massive for the community.
Could integrate with the existing invite system so direct-join still works alongside the browser.
Quest stage sync is fire-and-forget on the host side — there's no acknowledgement from the client. Need to add a two-phase commit here.
Reproduced during the Companions main quest. Both players got different objectives simultaneously. Modlog attached.
Tested on a fresh save — event triggers fine on year 1 and 2. Year 3+ it silently skips. No error in SMAPI log.
Found it: trigger condition checks Game1.year <= 2. Should be open-ended. Pushing fix today.
Can confirm — tested after removing the year cap from the condition and the event fired correctly on year 4.
Seconding this. There's a lot of content to track and the journal doesn't surface relationship milestones well at all.
The water breathing power is toggled on enter/exit water events, but the exit event doesn't fire on shallow beach tiles. So it stays active on land.
Confirmed on both Fabric 0.15 and 0.16. The exit water event fires correctly in deep water but not at sand-edge transitions.
Collected 5 screenshots from different users — the flash occurs consistently on the frame the teleport position resolves. Seems like a draw-order issue.
Deferring the particle spawn by 1 tick post-teleport resolves the flash in my test environment. Submitting a PR.
Tested in a world with Fire Resistance pots and in Nether Wastes — lava deals full damage regardless of Blazeborn immunity flag being set.
Recorded footage: the cliff charge triggers in every arena with a drop >3 units. Lhutel's pathfinding doesn't check the navigation mesh bounds before committing to the charge animation.
Adding an edge-detection pre-check before the charge commit. Should prevent the issue across all arena types.
Also observed this with the Banished Knight summon in the Farum Azula arena. Might be a shared pathfinding base class issue.
Reproduced with a minimal test save: single asteroid miner, 1000 bots at full capacity. Output queue hits zero yield after about 3 minutes of saturation.
Found a branch in the output allocation logic that exits early when belt-side capacity is full — it returns 0 instead of queuing the overflow. Classic off-by-one.

Benchmarked at 1000, 2500, and 5000 nodes. UPS is fine at 1000, starts degrading at 2500, and halves at 5000. The tick update loop iterates every node each tick regardless of whether they're connected to active research.
Implementing a dirty-flag system so only nodes adjacent to the active frontier get re-evaluated. Should bring the cost back to O(active_nodes) instead of O(total_nodes).
The rocket logistics bottleneck becomes brutal late-game. An orbital cannon with cooldown-based rate limiting would be a natural endgame progression unlock.
On Easy, the phase 2 transition triggers at full HP immediately upon entering the arena. Tested 3 times — consistent. Doesn't occur on Normal or Expert.

The heist NPC actors don't receive their moveto command until the ambient event's second tick. On the first tick they just stand at the spawn origin — then if a player sees them they permanently freeze.
Adding a moveto-target call immediately after NPC initialization in the spawn script. Testing across all 4 heist variants now.
Stack trace points to TunnelerMeme.Apply() calling into PainIdeology.GetModifier() which returns null when the modifier hasn't been initialized yet. Simple null guard should fix it.
Frame-by-frame analysis shows the hitbox lags behind the sprite by ~3 frames during the flurry. The hitbox is still using a static bounding box tied to the root bone instead of the weapon bone.
Switching to bone-based collision for that attack phase. Will need to test across all three attack variants in phase 2.
Confirmed: Enrage Mask is only reachable through the Uumuu gate in the current item graph. No alternative path exists. This makes the randomizer unsolvable in some seeds.
Verified: the bioluminescent creature spawn table has depth set to 0..50 instead of 200..400. Looks like a copy-paste from the shallow reef biome config.
Profiled the biome tile updater — it runs a full adjacency check on every tile every tick. With 4+ biome types this creates significant overlap and redundant checks at biome boundaries.
Confirmed: Emberveil's phase config has phase_threshold: 0.65. Should be 0.50. Trivial fix but needed a live reproduction to confirm the value.
The importer throws a silent exception when it encounters an unknown item ID string. It catches and swallows the error without any user feedback. Need to both fix the item map and surface the error.
A DPS calculator with ailment scaling would be genuinely complex to implement correctly — ignite, poison, and bleed all have different application models. Worth a design doc first.
Agreed. I'll draft the ailment DPS model spec. We can start with ignite since it's the simplest and build from there.
Reproduced with 8-party save: long rest at camp, Shadowheart in slot 5. After rest, she no longer moves toward player on area transition. Other companions in slots 1-4 are unaffected.
The follow-state register only iterates slots 1-4 after rest. Party expansion added slots 5-8 but the rest handler wasn't updated. Simple range fix.

Measured at 60fps: Friede's scythe roll-catch active window is 8 frames, but vanilla would be 6 at this weight class. The extra 2 frames create a consistent catch that isn't humanly avoidable with standard roll timing.
This one is delicate — too short and it becomes unviable, too long and it's oppressive. Will model against vanilla Friede data before patching.

Recorded Darkdrift L2 at the fog wall in 10 PvP sessions. Hit register fails on 7 of 10 attempts when the phantom's model clips the fog wall hitbox. Seems the wall collision is blocking the backstab trace.
The city plan validator runs on every autosave and overwrites any blueprint not in the approved plan list. There's no way to mark a custom blueprint as exempt currently.
Adding a custom_override flag to the blueprint schema. When set, the autosave hook will skip the plan validator for that cell. Will need migration for existing saves.
Tested with 32-plot settlement: framerate drops from 60 to 38 at NPC schedule shift. The shift fires all assignment updates synchronously on the main thread — takes ~180ms per shift.
Moving the assignment loop to a background worker. Schedule shifts will now complete off-thread with a callback that posts state back to the main thread in batches.
